entry.title: Re: How can I deploy my completed SAP Build Apps web appl...
entry.content: Look for failed network calls, CORS errors, or 404/500 from services

entry.title: Re: SAC: Story filter not working when set to single sele...
entry.content:
Dear @BeatrizDuenas,

I faced a similar issue in the past were # - unassigned was the only booked member of my dimension and for single selection filters, at least one booked member must be available, otherwise the single selection filter won't be able to render.

You can easily test by selecting the # - unassigned and see if the issue still persists.

I hope this helps.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Re: How can I deploy my completed SAP Build Apps web appl...
entry.content: Also hope the destinations are maintained properly->check connection(success)

entry.title: Re: Missing steps in "Create and Edit the Automation" Tut...
entry.content: Thanks Rohit 🙂

entry.title: Re: Navigate in RAP via Button from root to subnode, not...
entry.content:
If you are referring this navigation in UI then yes , you can Object page in order to show the details of the subnode (Child)  . 

Check this 

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/getting-started-with-creating-an-sap-fiori-elements-app-based-on-an-odata-v4-rap-service

entry.title: Re: Report S_PL0_86000030 showing incorrect values
entry.content:
• Did you get similar result with FAGLB03?
  • What did you already look for?
  • Execute a SQL and Abap trace, which tables were read and was some custome Enhencement executed

entry.title: Re: On Premise / Private Cloud Fiori Launchpad - News in New My Home
entry.content: Hi rb, Thanks @ThomasReiss for notifying me about this query. This blog provides information on the news feed https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/system-news-customizing-the-my-home-news-feed/ba-p/13579801. You can configure the visibility and the source of the news feed. The news feed can be set up to display 1. SAP System News (via excel) or 2. An RSS feed (url). The news tile is displayed only if the news URL is from the same domain. Thanks, Caroline

entry.title: Re: U: Error or Bug in Analytics Cloud Live Model Paramet...
entry.content:
We have:
BI_CONT        757 0034 SAPK-75734INBICONT     Business Intelligence Content
BI_CONT_XT 757 0034 SAPK-75734INBICONTXT Business Intelligence Content for Bobj I

We added to RSADMIN:
RSMD_ALLOW_SELOPT_OR_FOR1 LESEKLASSEA
RSMD_ALLOW_SELOPT_OR_FOR2 MERKMALB

Than, as a precaustion measure, delete the OLAP Cache:
RSRCACHE - OLAP: Cache Monitor

Restarted AfO and SAC:
... and it worked out

It might be that deleteing the OLAP Cache only could also work out

Thank You
Best Martin

entry.title: How to Exclude Reservation from MD04
entry.content:
Hello,

I’m trying to find a way to exclude specific reservations from appearing in MD04.

The use case is based on a condition in the reservation (when RESB-XWAOK is initial). In such cases, we want to prevent the reservation from being considered in MRP and ideally have it not show up in MD04 at all.

While researching possible enhancements, I came across the BAdI MD_CHANGE_MRP_DATA.

My question is: Is it possible to use MD_CHANGE_MRP_DATA to exclude reservation entries from MD04? If so, what’s the recommended approach? If not, is there another BAdI or enhancement point that would be more suitable for this requirement?

Any suggestions or guidance would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Complex selection not supported for filter search function
entry.content:
Kindly check also the Question, Solution and Comments in

U: Error or Bug in Analytics Cloud Live Model Parameter F4 Help for ID or description search

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Complex selection not supported for filter search function
entry.content: NONE

entry.title: Enter incoming invoice RPA
entry.content:
Dear all,   

we are implementing process Enter Incoming Invoice (PDF) - ECC (MIRO) (https://api.sap.com/package/3f1e226f28d84f309e7b0cf07579e5bd/overview), 

but unfortunately this error occurs when the agent is looking for the email containing the invoice (pls see the attached image).

Can someone help us?

Thank you.

Elena

I wanted to get an overview / understanding /Process around implementing Credit management in SAP S/4 PERSON_NAME by utilizing AI or Machine Learning on predictive analysis and what processes can be automated in SAP FSCM _ Credit management.

 

 

best regards

Srivalli

Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: AI or Machine Learning Implementation on SAP Credit m...
entry.content: The Predictive Capabilities in SAP Credit Management can be found in the SAP Help Portal Predictive Capabilities in SAP Credit Management

entry.title: Integrating SHL for assessments, but use SAP AI features for SHL skills at a later time?
entry.content:
Hello!

 

I'm integrating SHL for assessments with our RCM recruiting module and may add skills in the future. Can we use SAP AI-assisted features for SHL skills?

Thanks,

Kelly Conklin

entry.title: Re: Hey, I'm new here ^^
entry.content:
Thank you so much. I am very excited to be part of SAP community. 

I will definitely check it out. Thank you again.

entry.title: Re: Project System
entry.content:
A great place to start learning SAP PS is here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/4dd8cb7b1c484b4b93af84d00f60fdb8/1ad4b65334e6b54ce10000000a174cb4.html

Revenue Recognition is called Results Analysis, it is part of PS but also in many other areas of SAP.

PS projects can be made up of WBS Elements and/or Networks, look in the help for more details.

PS links to Materials Management several different ways.  Look in SAP help for more details.

entry.title: Re: Question: Is ABAP performance tracing (ST12) still a...
entry.content: Hi holm, i see your point. I just returned to the classical ABAP world and work on HANA and CDS topics, so not much experience on the topics you mentioned. How do you approach these topics today? What tools are you using? Kind regards, Hermann

entry.title: Native HANA / XSA CDS - annotation for 'measure'
entry.content:
Hi experts,

   working on a HANA 1.0 (1.120) on premise Database and looking into what we can achieve directly on the DB for applications not running an ABAP layer.

Is it possible to define a column for a Native HANA CDS as a Measure, allowing the OData service to effectively request a dynamic "group by" based on the columns (Dimensions) requested in the query?

Example:

TestCDS.hdbdd file that does not define the Measure;

namespace IHA.models.MII.TAB_VIEWS.TD.services;
@SCHEMA: 'SAPD33'
context TestCDS {
view TestCDS as select from RESB
{
RSNUM,
RSPOS,
AUFNR,
MATNR,
BDMNG
};
};

 

Ideally I want to define BDMNG as a measure, however replacing this with @DefaultAggregation: #SUM BDMNG just results in an error when saving via the HANA Web IDE

 

The OData service is defined as;

service namespace "pp.mii.resb" {
"IHA.models.MII.TAB_VIEWS.TD.services::TestCDS.TestCDS"
as "reservationdata" keys generate local "ID"
;
}
annotations {
enable OData4SAP;
}

 

I read that it may also be possible to define the expression within the OData service (https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/advanced/odata#expression-annotations) but I cannot see how to achieve this within my OData defintion.

 

Am I attempting something only possible in an ABAP CDS/OData service or is this possible directly at DB level?

entry.title: SAP MDK Web - Debug Sources
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I developed an SAP MDK web application that I would now like to debug the local deployment. While the debug sources for my own code are available in the developer console, I would also like to access the debug sources of the MDK web runtime. This would help me better understand the runtime itself and the interaction between the runtime and my application code

Is there a way to enable the debug sources of the SAP MDK Web Runtime?

Any help is greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content:
Hi Sandra_Rossi,

I implemented an enhancement to retrieve my data in the end of CU50. (LCKDIO33 in the end of PERFOM fill_costgrid_table ).

All data that I need are, in the enhancement, insert into a Z table.

But the SHDB does not allow me to make the calculation of the CU50 and therefore does not allow me to go through my enhancement and therefore does not record any entry in my Z table.

I tried to play with all options of the SHDB -> without success

Can you please help me. 
Thanks in advance

entry.title: Sales Order Invoice Request is with status Inconsistent and corresponding Invoice not processed
entry.content:
Sales Order Invoice Request is with status Inconsistent and due to that the Outbound Delivery Invoice Request cannot be processed. 

When click on Check button, system shows the error "Financial data for seller missing in master data of account"


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Sales Order Invoice Request is with status Inconsiste...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

 

When the Invoice Request was created the customer account did not have the company maintained in the Financial Data tab, due to that the issue is displayed. 

To resolve the issue kindly follow the KBA 1822783 - Inconsistent Invoice Request is Created With Error Message: "Financial data for seller missing in master data of account"

 

Thanks & Regards,

Arjun T H

SAP Product Support

entry.title: How to automate creation of Inbound delivery, GR, WT/WO for Putaway - public edition EWM
entry.content:
Hi experts, 

currently we are looking for automatic Warehouse processes for SAP EWM, which incl. automatically creation of inbound delivery goods receipt, warehouse tasks/orders for putaway, because we're preparing to combine processes with warehouse operator app, without doing above mentioned steps manually as before.  

As much as we know the current release Warehouse operator App 2.4 could only create HU WT/WO but not product WT/WO.

SAP Warehouse Operator Version 2.4 - What's new? - SAP Community

Kindly adivce, thanks in advacne.

regards,

Xiawei

entry.title: Sap Convergent Invoicing Process
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I need to carry out the following task in Convergent Invoicing:

Insert a zeroing line in the invoice, after the invoice has been calculated including VAT. I need the following information:

- Where to position myself within Convergent Invoicing in order to have the completed invoice including VAT. 

- Which standard functionality in Convergent Invoicing to call in order to write to DFKKOP and DFKKOPK, to insert the new zeroing invoice line and its posting

Example:
Total amount in Convergent Invoicing: €0.02

New line added via custom event: -€0.02
= Updated total amount: €0.00

Laszlo

entry.title: Re: Different number ranges for sales document type and b...
entry.content:
Hi @AshishTumpally ,

please check the functionality Flexible Sales Document Numbering in S/4Hana Cloud. This functionality also supports custom fields in the sales document header, which is the case in your scenario. 

Best regards

entry.title: Embed a SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) story into a SharePoint
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We're trying to embed a SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) story into a SharePoint site using the Embed web part. Both SharePoint and SAC use SAML-based Single Sign-On (SSO) via Azure AD, and user authentication works as expected.

Here's the embed code we're using:

<iframesrc="https: //XXXXXXXX.euXX.hcs.cloud.sap/sap/fpa/ui/tenants/XXXXXXX/bo/embed/story/XXXXXXXXXX" width="100%" height="600"></iframe>

What we've already done:

  • In SharePoint:
    Under Site Settings > HTML Field Security, we've added the domain:
    XXXXXXXX.euXX.hcs.cloud.sap
  • In Azure AD:
    SAP Analytics Cloud has been added as an enterprise application.
  • In SAC (under System > Administration > App Integration):
    We've added the following trusted domains:
    • https: //XXXXXXX.sharepoint.com
    • https://*.sharepoint.com

      Problem:

      When we try to view the embed web part in SharePoint, we get an error inside the iFrame. We’ve attached a screenshot of the error below for reference.

      Question:

      Has anyone successfully embedded a SAC story in SharePoint this way?

      Are there any additional configurations needed in SAC (e.g. headers, CSP, embedding permissions) or SharePoint (web part limitations, CSP settings, etc.)?

      Any help or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

      Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Insert Link feature stopped working in SEN
entry.content: SAP will apply a hotfix to Cloud SEN during the weekend of April 12. The hotfix file will be available for On-Premise customers in https://me.sap.com.

entry.title: Re: Issue with OData Requests in Analytical Models Follow...
entry.content: We've had the same problem since the weekend.

entry.title: Re: Open Items Display in FBL1N but F-44 is not taking th...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please refer to SAP note: 1709866

In the note it says:

There can be 4 possibles raisons:

a) Check if the clearing date on the initial screen of F-44/F-32 is equal to or later than the posting date of the items.

b) Check if the items have a special GL indicator on them, and if so, deselect the "Normal OI" flag on the initial screen of F-44/F-32.

c) Check that the items are not included in a payment proposal that has not yet been paid. You can check in table REGUH  to see if these items are in there. If so, then you should either complete the payment step in F110 or else delete the proposal via F110 to "free up" these items for manual clearing.

You can check REGUP with doc number to find which Proposal contain the GL doc number

d) Check if document Central Finance (CFIN) is used but not the CFIN function Central Payment.

 

Hope it'll help.

entry.title: Re: EC Time - Tipps and hints Nr. 5: how to accrual vacation time accounts based on EC time sheet da
entry.content:
Hi @Volker_Ruof, 

Thanks for the in-depth guide on this subject. What if we wanted to achieve something like this, but only considering attendance times during calendar OFF days, for example: for every hour worked in an OFF day, 30 minutes of vacation are added. What would be the correct approach to creating this? Tried some different configurations, but couldn't achieve it.

Thanks in advance,

Luís

entry.title: Re: Not able to update the values in S4Hana public with PUT method
entry.content:
Hi Kaito2,

Could you provide a few more details?

We assume you are using this api: https://api.sap.com/api/CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001/resource/Purchase_Order

correct? (looks like yes from your screen shot). - First thought is you should be using a PATCH and not PUT - i don't see an operation for PUT.

Is the custom field enabled for the API?

If you do a GET immediately after you do the PATCH, is the custom field updated?

I have seen if you are trying to refresh immediately in the UI, it does not look updated right away due to cache. - Could you try to close the app and open it again?

Whereas the GET should show the change immediately. More info about how you are trying to validate the success.

How are you identifying the PO you want to update? It is not clear in the API call you are making and it is not in the body.

I don't think you need the API Key but could be wrong. I assume you got this from the Business Accelerator Try Out feature.

When you mentioned you were successful in the Try Out feature, did you put in your system specifics in the Environments area?

There is an app Odata Error Log or similar name (try just Odata) - might interesting to see if there is any info there.

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE HADR - database replication limit
entry.content: I usually setup clients with master database replication if simply to insure logins, passwords and login roles are kept in sync with the remote system; this assumes the target dataserver's replicate databases are coming from the same source dataserver (ie, the source and target dataservers are managing the same set of logins).

entry.title: How Does the Integration of SAP S/4HANA Modules Enhance Operational Efficiency for Businesses?
entry.content:
How does the integration of the finance, supply chain, and production planning modules in the SAP S/4HANA platform optimize data flow and decision-making processes? What are the advantages provided by this integration?

https://www.researchgate.net/spotlight/67f7c456fd4a6077420b6bfa

entry.title: Re: How can I assign operation from one resource to two or three resources using the “SAP_MULT_SCH”?
entry.content: Hi! I´m in the same situation, do you have any solution? Thank you!

entry.title: How to add same HTML5 Application with different semantic object in SAP Build Work zone (Launchpad)
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have a question when we deploy the application on cloud foundry using BAS then HTML5 content app generated and when i wanted to register this application on Launchpad service then all fields are auto filled.

I would like to change the semantic object of navigation intent of this. and want to register the same application with different semantic object for multiple roles.

zzThequeastion is using the same application with different semantic object for different roles.

 

Appreciate you help

entry.title: Re: SDMI CL_SDM_BUT0BK_V2
entry.content:
Hi LEE325

i cleaned up the table V_BUT100_EMP and did a retry - unfortunately with no success.
SAP Support still does not answer to my topic.
Did you see the class CL_SDM_BUT0BK_V2 in your "sdm_mon" Transaction ? In 2022 i can not see this class.
Did you do something else except cleaning the table ?

Thanks and best regards

entry.title: CDS View for Invoice Related Dispute
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

We are  trying to get a CDS view to extract data in Datasphere on Invoice Related Dispute.
We are not able to find one Release version of CDS View which we can use to create Custom CDS View and pull it in Datasphere.
There is another View in View Browser as R_DisputeCaseLinkedInvoice , which seems to work for us but it is not released.

Please suggest one such view or resolution for us.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CDS View for Invoice Related Dispute
entry.content: CDS view I_DisputeCaseLinkedObject can be used to provide information about linked objects of a dispute case. You can refer to the SAP Help Portal Linked Objects of a Dispute Case . You can also check in the Help Portal other CDS views for Dispute Resolution available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

entry.title: Error during ADT Installation because of missing
entry.content:
Dear Community Members,

I am writing to you with a very basic issue but whatever I try it does not seem to be resolved.
Whenever I try to install ADT via the URL listed in the installation guide (link below) I receive the following error:

Cannot complete the install because one or more required items could not be found.

Software being installed: ABAP Business Object Tools (Developer Edition) 1.183.0 (com.sap.adt.tools.bopf.devedition.feature.group 1.183.0)

Missing requirement: BOPF Business Object Plug-in 1.183.0 (com.sap.adt.bopf.businessobject 1.183.0) requires 'osgi.bundle; org.eclipse.emf.workspace [1.6.0,2.0.0)' but it could not be found

Cannot satisfy dependency:

From: ABAP Business Object Tools (Developer Edition) 1.183.0 (com.sap.adt.tools.bopf.devedition.feature.group 1.183.0)

To: org.eclipse.equinox.p2.iu; com.sap.adt.tools.bopf.feature.group [1.183.0,1.183.0]

Cannot satisfy dependency:

From: BOPF Business Object Tools 1.183.0 (com.sap.adt.tools.bopf.feature.group 1.183.0)

To: org.eclipse.equinox.p2.iu; com.sap.adt.bopf.businessobject [1.183.0,1.183.0]

Link to installation guide:  Download the Eclipse IDE and add the ABAP Development Tools (ADT) Plugin | SAP Tutorials
I even tried to solve the issue by removing the bundled JustJRE from eclipse and altering the eclipse.ini file as described in the relevant note.
JDK 24 is installed as well.

Sap Relase S/HANA 2020 FPS01
Client OS: Windows 11 Enterprise
Eclipse and ADT both latest releases

Thank you for your help in advance!

Best Regards,
Tamas

entry.title: Re: Replacement of Core Tool functionalities by functionalities in Cockpit Fiori apps as of SAP CC 2
entry.content:
Hello Grace, 

Thanks for reaching out to us and sharing your feedback. We certainly acknowledge this, and it is on our roadmap to support existing charge plans in Cockpit. Please refer to our Roadmap Explorer for more details.

Moreover, as mentioned, this will be a gradual shift for customers upgrading to 2025 FPS0. We plan to gradually replace features from core tol to cockpit, starting with the User Administrator role.

Regards
Abhijeet

entry.content:
Hello Łukasz,

please check Flexible Sales Document Numbering functionality for S/4Hana Cloud.

Best regards

entry.title: API to create Inbound Delivery with reference to PO
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to use the API - API_INBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV/A_InbDeliveryHeader to create the inbound delivery with reference to a Purchase Order. I am using the following Json in the Gateway client.

But, I get the following error in the log.

Appreciate any inputs on what I am doing wrong here.

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Compensation Lookup table movement
entry.content:
Hi @SF_Abhisek 

You can use instance sync (built-in) or package configuration manager (requires certification) to automatically transfer lookup tables from one instance to another (as well as business rules used for eligibility).

If you don't use one of these two options there is no possibility to mass download and mass reimport all lookup tables through CSV files, it must be done one by one.

 

All the best,
Xavier

entry.title: Re: SDMI CL_SDM_BUT0BK_V2
entry.content:
Hi LEE325,

How did you changed the protect field, could you help me?

entry.title: Re: Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) Artifact with SAP Cloud Transport Management
entry.content:
Hi Artur,

Yes, you need two CAS instances, one for each source tenant/subaccount.

Let me clarify the setup:

  • You have a central administrative subaccount that runs cTMS. Depending on the number of target Cloud Integration Tenants, this subaccount needs multiple destinations configured—typically for each target tenant (e.g., QA new, QA old, PRD new, PRD old).
    Each destination corresponds to a transport node, as defined and used in cTMS.

  • You also have two source subaccounts (DEV new and DEV old), each running a Cloud Integration Tenant along with a dedicated CAS instance.
    Within each of these source subaccounts, you'll need to configure the following three destinations:

    1. TransportManagementService – points to the cTMS in the central subaccount

    2. ContentAssemblyService – points to the CAS instance in the same subaccount

    3. Cloud Integration – points to the Integration Suite in the same subaccount

Hope this answer helped. You may also check our latest tutorial which is describing this scenario: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/btp-transport-management-cpi-01-use-case.html. Please note: in this tutorial we use CAS also at the target, which is just another option.

All the best,

Sven

entry.title: Re: Different number ranges for sales document type and b...
entry.content: FYI The blog refers to this online help: Flexible Sales Document Numbering.

entry.title: Mashup for Custom View
entry.content:
Hello All,

KBA "2707848 - How to Add Different Types of Mashups in Cloud for Customer" explains how to add mashup.

All help/description I found explains how to add mashup via adaptation.

But what if I do have custom object and for TI screen I would like to add to one of the tabs Mashup ?

Is there any way to do it?

entry.title: Re: Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) Artifact with SAP Cloud Transport Management
entry.content:
Hi Sven
Thank you for answer. Helped. After creation CAS in CPI DEV NEW all is ok.

https://developers-qa-blue.wcms-nonprod.c.eu-de-2.cloud.sap/tutorials/btp-transport-management-cpi-01-use-case.html isn't available.

 

Looks internal SAP document.

entry.title: Re: ADT Keyword Highlights Are Broken
entry.content:

thanks for posting the question. I experience different but similar problems with RAP syntax highlighting (newest ADT version):

It would be great if this could be fixed.

entry.title: Re: How to create a OData Service in Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi iamnaveen

Could you provide some more information about your use case?

For example, you can use the Public APIs already provided: 

https://api.sap.com/products/SAPS4HANACloud/apis/all

If you want to update something, you will need to use x-csrf-token cross site scripting token:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/s-4hana-cloud-x-csrf-token-and-e-tag-validation/ba-p/13427700

Or you can create a custom CDS View and expose it as Odata (for Read only)
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/f4dcfd6e14b54177b98d6099ddb2014e.html?locale=en-US

If you want to create your own custom APIs and expose them, you can investigate Developer Extensibility:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/e1059ff581854a699f15734049f14293.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/9e6f18e492de47fea08a818f5c3225ac.html?locale=en-US

https://api.sap.com/products/SAPS4HANACloud/onstackextensibility/bointerface

 

thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) Artifact with SAP Cloud Transport Management
entry.content: Please use this link for the tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/btp-transport-management-cpi-01-use-case.html

entry.title: Re: Complementar de preço sem quantidade
entry.content: Boa tarde

entry.title: Re: Navigate in RAP via Button from root to subnode, not via standard arrow
entry.content: yes, but why not standard?

entry.title: Generic OData Service - Datasphere Integration
entry.content:
Hello all,

Welcome back again to my series of blog posts on various objects in the SAP datasphere. In this blog post, we will see how Generic OData Service - Datasphere Integration works.

It is easy in SAP Datasphere to connect to a generic OData service. There may be a question of why we need to integrate OData service with SAP Datasphere, but it depends on the business case. 

In earlier days, to send SAP data to the outside world, we used to create files stored in the AL directory, or we used to create open hub destinations, which further needed to be connected to third-party ETL tools like SAP BODS, etc., to load to a third-party system.

Then came the OData service concept, and we can generate OData on CDS views, ADSOs, and even on Bex queries (conditions apply) and can send the SAP data to the outside world.

Now let us get set to go!

To start with, I will go with the generic OData service, which is readily available publicly on the internet.

Odata service URL : https://services.odata.org/V4/NORTHWIND/NORTHWIND.SVC/ 

The OData service is working as expected. Now test this odate service in Postman and see if it works. It sh

So this OData service is working and tested perfectly. it supports only GET methods. Cool!

Now let us go to our SAP datasphere tenant and try to establish a connection. SAP DataSphere provides excellent connectivity to the majority of stakeholders to bring the data into SAP DataSphere with a data federation mechanism. As of now there are 43 different connections I have seen, including Azure, AWS, Google BigQuery in GCP, and even Alibaba Cloud.

Now see!

Now go to SAP data sphere then Connectionsand choose "Generic Odata" connection. Please find the below picture.

Give the relevant details and click on save. As this OData service does not require any authentication, it is publicly available, and we do not need to give any authentication details such as a user name, host, or password, etc.

 Once we save, the connection is successfully established with the Odata service.

Validate and see the result.

Real-time replication status is not applicable as we are consuming the API in real time only.

The point to be noted here is for a generic Odata service Data flows, remote tables are supported, but replication flows are not supported.

So now we're all set, and we consumed the API in SAP Datasphere.

Now let us go how it looks like. Let us go to dataflow area in data builder and try to create a data flow on the entities in the API.

 

Now, as we see the oDATA service API is available in SAP datasphere and we can create a dataflow or graphical view or SQL view on top of the tables.

This is why we can perform Generic OData Service - Datasphere Integration

Thank you,

Narasingha

entry.title: Re: SAP® Extended Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA 2021 – What’s New
entry.content:
@Jörg_Michaelis on 2022 Jan 12 9:50 AM, you mentioned that advanced integration between PM and EWM with PMR was not on the roadmap.
Is this still the case today? Or is there a possibility to create PMR for PM orders?

entry.title: Re: Integration of Plant Maintenance(PM) with Extended warehouse management (EWM) in S/4HANA 2021 Re
entry.content:
In this post it is mentioned that batch information is not required in MB26:

SAP® Extended Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA ... - SAP Community

(see answer of the author Jorg Michaelis on 2022 Jan 12 9:50 AM)

entry.title: Re: Change credit limit and extend credit limit validity...
entry.content: Job Update Credit Limit for Business Partners in app Schedule Credit Management Jobs can be used to mass update the credit limit for business partners by triggering a re-evaluation of the rules for calculating the credit limit for business partners. Pre-requisites for availing the Option 2 job template and Option 3 Credit Management Master Data API is that the Advanced Credit Management (1QM) scope item is activated in your system.

entry.title: Re: Automatic adjustment of the planned delivery date of...
entry.content:
Hello

MRP does not consider the component delivery date when scheduling a planned order. MRP will always plan the parent materials first, and there is no information about the component replenishment when creating a planned order, so the logic you are expecting does not exist in MRP. 

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Re: API to create Inbound Delivery with reference to PO
entry.content:
Check those samples in this SAP online help: Create Inbound Delivery, look for

  • Create an inbound delivery from purchase order 4500003742
  • Create an inbound delivery from a distinct purchase order item

(Basically use 'ReferenceSDDocument')

entry.title: FPM: Custom Action on Table Navigating to Freestyle View – Routing Issues
entry.content:
Hey everyone 👋,

I'm currently working on a Fiori elements application using the Flexible Programming Model (FPM). Here's what I'm trying to achieve:

I'd like to add a custom action button on a Table Building Block (e.g. <macros:Table>) that allows the user to navigate to a freestyle view. The goal is to show a custom-made UI5 XML view, which contains complex functionality that doesn't fit into a standard Fiori elements layout.

I’ve already implemented the action using the press handler in the table, and the logic itself is working. The problem arises when I try to set up navigation to the freestyle view using routes and targets in the manifest.json.

As soon as I define the new route/target for the freestyle view the application fails to launch entirely, and I get no detailed error messages in the console that help pinpoint the issue.

Has anyone successfully:

  • Added a freestyle view to an FPM-based app?

  • Navigated from a building block (e.g. table action) to that freestyle view?

  • Modified the manifest.json routing correctly to support this?

Any code snippets or working examples would be incredibly appreciated 🙏

Thanks a lot in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK Web - Debug Sources
entry.content: No, we do not provide the source for the MDK Web Runtime.  Is there a particular question you have regarding the runtime?

entry.title: Re: Improve Q&A to show "SAP managed tags" and "labels" a...
entry.content: Thank you @Sandra_Rossi. I have taken note and added it to our change request list (that soon will be shared with all of you).

entry.title: Do you know how to use Fringe Benefits Tax (FBT) in Concur Expense Professional Edition?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Working with Fringe Benefits Tax (FBT) in Concur Expense Professional Edition learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey helps you gain a solid understanding of using Fringe Benefits Tax (FBT) in Concur Expense Professional Edition.

Learning objectives
At the end of this learning journey, administrators and consultants will be able to, in Concur Expense Professional Edition:

  • Describe FBT and why tracking it is essential to your business
  • Explain to your users how to complete an expense report that contains FBT-related expenses
  • Identify the considerations for users when they are subject to FBT
  • Compare Attendee Status and Attendee Types
  • Configure the Attendee Types to support FBT
  • Configure FBT in the Tax Administration tool
  • Configure the Attendee Detail View to support FBT
  • Activate FBT on your Australia Policy
  • Activate the Travel Diary on your Australia Policy
  • Explain how FBT affects SAE

Prerequisites
Learners should have a firm understanding of how Concur Expense operates as a business user.
Please complete the Getting Started with Concur Expense for Business Users learning journey before completing this course.

Please post you question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Multi resource scheduling heuristics SAP_MULT_SCH error
entry.content: Hi! In my case, it doesn't work but, it is discrete, not PPPI. Do you know if these heuristic works for discrete production or only process? Thank you!!

entry.title: We need to know how many times a transaction have been used by users.
entry.content:
We need to know the count of the Transaction codes used. like in last 3 months how many times the same transaction have been used?
Is there any T-Code/table to identify such scenario? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: We need to know how many times a transaction have bee...
entry.content:
This is not the sort of information that is stored for long in the system by default.

My suggestion would be to use the Security Audit Log.

This can record a lot of useful data including transaction usage for users.

This, however, needs to be set up using rsau_config/SM19.

So you cannot read past data if this was not previously configured.

Data can be read in rsau_read_log/SM20.

entry.title: Re: SapUniversalID is Case-Sensitive on the User-Name?!
entry.content: Hi @joachimrees1 Where exactly did this happen? In your community profile settings?

entry.title: Re: Navigate in RAP via Button from root to subnode, not via standard arrow
entry.content: Hi Junwu, the user has to create the subnode of the root, with a button you can give the button a name so that the user knows he has to click on it to create the subnode, when you have only an arrow, the user doesn't know where to create the subnode. (it's a root with a lot of kids, so not only 1 root and 1 subnode)

entry.title: Production Versions in Sub-contracting Inforecord
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

We have created two production versions (0001 and 0002) for a sub-contracting material. There is only one vendor for this material, and we have updated the corresponding info record to version 0002. However, the MRP is still selecting version 0001 when generating purchase requisitions. Could you please advise on why this might be happening?

Thank you for your assistance.

System: S/4 HANA

entry.title: Re: SAC: Story filter not working when set to single sele...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you so much, it was because of what you mentioned 

Regards

Beatriz

entry.title: Re: RAP Create button still enabled after disabling the f...
entry.content: I struggled with the same requirement, conditionally hide the Create button. I did not manage to do this, but got something which works the same. By using method get_global_features() you can conditionally say that the user is not authorized, and block him/her from creating an new entry.

entry.title: Re: Multi-resource scheduling heuristic SAP_MULT_SCH problem
entry.content: Hi! Could you help me? I woult like to know if this heuristics works for PP (discrete manufacturing). I'm having problemn with it and maybe is for that reason. Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Understand the Cost and Pricing Aspects for Your Industry Cloud Solution.
entry.content: Thanks for sharing your insight @finn_backer !

entry.title: Re: RAP Create button still enabled after disabling the f...
entry.content: To do this, do the following:

entry.title: Re: RAP Create button still enabled after disabling the f...
entry.content: To do this, do the following: 1. Define create in your behaviour definition as: create ( features : global ); 2. Define this method in the Local Types part of your implementation class like this: METHODS get_global_features FOR GLOBAL FEATURES IMPORTING REQUEST requested_features FOR MissionNeeds RESULT result. 3. Implement like this: result-%create = COND #( WHEN sy-subrc = 0 THEN if_abap_behv=>auth-allowed ELSE if_abap_behv=>auth-unauthorized ).

entry.title: A journey from Netweaver 7.50 to S/4HANA - Part 2: What's Cooking?
entry.content:
Find part 1 here to learn more about our project in general.

Most of the activities mentioned in this article are related to what I called "Worldwide DEV" in the image used in part 1.

Creating Cookbooks

Based on the results from the latest S/4HANA Readiness Checks, we created a list of all 132 Simplification Item (SI) notes we had findings for and downloaded them as PDF files to our Google-Workspace. We wanted to have them available "locally" in order to try and get help from Google's Gemini KI with categorizing the SI's content.

We then assigned several SIs to each team member in our development team to read through them and summarize the gist in a "cookbook". For that, we concentrated on extracting information about how a finding should be fixed, taking note of available quick fixes and replacement options which already exist or may need to be created. Well aware that findings for which "no functional alternative available" were shown, would require involvement of our functional teams, we highlighted these SI separately and estimated (much) more time for fixing them.

In the case of "replace old code with this new code", we provided some examples which we put together with the help of a sandbox system converted to S/4HANA. Here is a random example how this looked like in a cookbook:

The idea behind the creation of the cookbooks is to provide a starting point for a developer fixing a program's S/4HANA Readiness findings during the manual code conversion and ideally make it unnecesary to read many SIs in detail.

Some preparatory activities in the NetWeaver Dev systems

As we are going to set up retrofit from the NW to the S/4HANA systems we used some available time to already create stand-alone Z* entities in our NetWeaver DEV-systems while waiting for the new S/4HANA DEV systems to become available. These objects are for example Z-copies of domains, data elements, structures, messages or even some function modules where we currently use standard objects from components like IS-Health which will no longer be available in S/4HANA (I had already published a blog post about those interesting findings way back in 2019!).

Speaking of which: anybody have some ideas how to replace memory IDs from IS-Health which will no longer exist in S/4HANA? It's one of the object types, I asked about in this question but haven't received any answers yet.

Tackling objects locked in open transports in the NetWeaver Dev system

After the future S/4HANA DEV system was created as a copy of the NetWeaver DEV system, we needed to take a closer look at objects locked in modifiable transports in the NetWeaver DEV system. As a result of the system copy, the objects were no longer locked to a transport but contained the unfinished - or even no longer needed -  changes applied in the NetWeaver DEV system. This could obviously cause issues if any of these objects required changes for S/4HANA and we didn't want to accidentally introduce potentially "half-baked" changes into what was going to eventually become our productive S/4HANA landscape!

We therefore had another preliminary task on our hands in the new DEV system before it got converted to S/4HANA: restore the earlier version of the impacted objects based on a list of open transports and the objects they contained. The restore was either accomplished by reverting back to the earlier version or by copying and pasting the code from the productive system.

Should any of these open changes be finished in the NetWeaver landscape, we'll get the new versions of the objects via retrofit into the S/4HANA world.

That's it for the 2nd leg of our journey to S/4HANA and it’s time for some rest around the campfire for our hikers!

Image generated with Gemini

Cheers

Bärbel

entry.title: Re: RAP Create button still enabled after disabling the f...
entry.content: This way, you can block the creation of an entry. Of course it would be better if you could make the button visible, but since RAP works with entities and here there is no entity I can understand that this is not easy to implement in RAP Fiori for SAP. In my opinion, this approach comes the closest to the desired behaviour.

entry.title: Re: API to create Inbound Delivery with reference to PO
entry.content:
Hello,

I tried checking this API in the SAP Sandbox system using TRY OUT feature in api.sap.com and it's indeed noted that there is no property/field available with name "ReferenceDocument" in EntitySet "A_InbDeliveryHeader" . You have passed 2 properties from header in this payload "ReferenceDocument" and "Supplier" , "Supplier" is there but the other one is not there and the message you got as a response is self-explanatory.

Please check.

Additional info:

"ReferenceDocument" is indeed found to be at the associated Item level entity that too with name "ReferenceSDDocument" , this api seems to be for Inbound Delivery to be created with respect to a Sales document and not for a Purchase document.

Thanks,

Sijin

entry.title: Re: Alternative Resource scheduling

entry.title: Re: How to attach more than two SCORM 2004 packages for one item?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Although this post explains a "workaround" on getting several SCORM 2004 packages to be added to one single item, this doesn't mean the contents will work appropriately when the users launch them.

The "workaround" explained above seems to be relying on a defect, because SCORM 2004 content should always be added as part of a package to the item and no item can have more than one SCORM 2004 content package added to it (since communication is happening on package level for this content type).

Not following this and creating an unsupported item content structure (adding more than one SCORM 2004 per item) will result in issues, like the behavior being reported by some of you at this post, because LMS expects the SCORM 2004 to be added to the item as only one content which is part of a single content parent package.

All of those content objects used in the item should be inside a single SCORM 2004 content within the same package, to be delivered this way by the content developer. Then the one .zip content can be imported as a single package, to be then associated to the item.

Please follow the best practices when creating content structures for items, as the end results will be different / unwanted when utilizing an unsupported setup/structure, and SAP will not be able to offer/support for unsupported use cases.

The SCORM 2004 limitations are explained in the following KBA as well:

2073540 - FAQ: Why do I get an error when trying to attach two SCORM 2004 packages to one item? - Learning

  • You cannot use two packages in the same item, yet SCORM 2004 requires that it be assigned via package; thus, you can only have one SCORM 2004 Package per item. 

Please note that LMS is designed according to the global SCORM content standards - which expects SCORM 2004 to be utilized with it's own parent package and without any other SCORM 2004 content in the same item -, so the LMS features will expect the content structure of an item to be set up according to the best practices. Any content issues, completion problems or unexpected behaviors are not reviewed by SAP Support if the customer is having an unsupported setup with SCORM 2004 content objects.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: How to blog in SAP Community
entry.content:
Welcome back @joachimrees1 🙂 

Our groups are moderated, so you need to patient and wait for a curator to get to your post.

entry.title: Re: RAP - Fiori Elements: Disable Create Button App, but not RAP functionality
entry.content: The attribute does not work. This only works dynamically for entities, and for Create button on empty list screen this is not there. However, there is an alternative posted here, where get_global_features is used, and the user is blocked when trying to create an entity. Not the same as disabling the button, but as close as can be achieved at this moment. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/rap-create-button-still-enabled-after-disabling-the-feature/qaq-p/13733494/comment-id/4835195#M4835195

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps for SAP Launchpad Service – Part 2
entry.content:
Hi @devbot 

ok then I will call it a reuse app. 
So you are going to open a new window which opens the html page of the reuse app which renders it.
The options for data transfer are URL parameters and java script communication.
As the URL of the reuse app will have the same domain as your main app, you should be able to do direct java script calls into the opened window. Otherwise you could do postMessage communication.

Anyway this question is more general about javascript development and outside of the scope of this blog.

Best regards
Matthias

entry.title: MRP Set up for multiple Storage Location in S/4 Hana
entry.content:
Hi All,
Can any one help me out how to Customize  2 storage locations into one MRP Area and run MRP with respect to that MRP area only. MRP to runned at storage location leve not at plant level

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule icon missing in S/4HNA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi @SCHNEIDERT ,

Have you tried to use Incognito version of a web browser?

Sometimes the caches in the web browser prevent you from showing everything, including Joule.

After the confirmation of showing Joule icon in Incognito version, you have to delete all cashes of your web browser to clean it up.

Regards,

George

entry.title: SAP CPI - Unable to parse hcicertificate using KeystoreService
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to integrate CPI with Microsoft Entra ID using client certificate credential. I tried using SAP's hcicertificate keypair in groovy script like 

Certificate cert = keystoreService.getCertificate("hcicertificate")

ByteArrayInputStream certificateStream = new ByteArrayInputStream(cert.getEncoded())

TokenCredential credential = new ClientCertificateCredentialBuilder().clientId(clientId).tenantId(tenantId).pfxCertificate(certificateStream).build();

But getToken from credential gives this error - "Failed to parse the certificate for the credential: DER input, Integer tag error"

I read hcicertificate is X.509 and this error means incorrect certificate format used. I tried with different .pfx keypair too but got same error.

Please can anyone help?

entry.title: Introducing Analytics for SAP BTP Cockpit to Enhance Your Experience
entry.content:
Great news! Starting April 17, we're integrating Analytics into the SAP BTP Cockpit to make your experience even better. But to do this, we first need the OK from you.

By giving us your consent, you’ll let our product teams gather anonymous product usage analytics data about how you interact with the BTP Cockpit. The data includes information such as the frequency of page visits, usage of features like buttons and links, time spent on each page, and the navigation paths users follow within the BTP Cockpit. Be assured that we won't collect any personal information – only the information we need to uncover where you're encountering challenges or spending extra time.

Your usage data is powerful. It helps our product teams make informed decisions and enhance the functionality and interfaces of BTP Cockpit. In short, it helps us to deliver a more satisfying experience for you.

 

How it Works

Analytics operates with a focus on user consent and anonymity. No data will be collected unless you give consent.

1. Customer consent by administrators: Consent is needed from a global account administrator for all users of a global account. For each global account, an administrator can choose whether to allow their users to participate in sending usage data.

The dialog to enable analytics is only visible to users with the global account administrator role and if consent has not already been granted for the current global account by another administrator.

The consent can be changed any time in the global account settings by the administrators.

2. User consent: In addition to the global account administrator, each individual user also must decide. Each user will see the cookie banner below, and by accepting all cookies, they opt into analytics. Usage data will only be sent if global account administrator and user consent are provided.

Users can change their consent at any time in the Cookie Preferences setting.

 
3. Data analysis: Product teams at BTP gain access to aggregated information, including most visited pages, time spent per page, and exit rates. These insights support the identification of issues and prioritization of platform improvements.
 

Data Security and Privacy

We understand the importance of data security and privacy, and have taken robust measures to safeguard your information:

No monetizing or advertising: Your data will never be used for monetizing or advertising purposes.

Full control: Global account administrators and individual users retain complete control over the activation of usage analytics. If you decide not to participate, your user experience will remain unchanged, and no anonymized data will be collected.

Anonymized data: All data collected is anonymized and stripped of personal or company-identifying information, ensuring privacy for all users.

Exclusion of application data: We do not collect any data related to your applications, including inputs you've made.

 

Summary

By enabling analytics, you contribute to a cycle of improvements that will benefit not only your own user experience but that of the entire SAP community. We are excited to bring this advancement to the SAP BTP Cockpit and look forward to making further enhancements based on invaluable user insights.

entry.title: Massive Creation of Version-Controlled Bill of Material (Version BOMs) in SAP PEO
entry.content:
Introduction

A bill of material (BOM) is a list of all components required in a process to make a product or assembly, together with the quantities required, the unit of measure, the description, the storage location, the production supply area, etc… The components are known as BOM items.

On a normal BOM, the different changes states are represented using date effectivity, so changes depend on dates. For BOMs with a change history, the change states are identified using the Engineering Change Numbers (ECN).

SAP Production Engineering and Operations (PEO) introduced a new version-controlled BOM concept, where different change states of the BOM are represented by versions. This means no need to work with ECN and date combinations. The main highlight of SAP PEO is then the use of Version BOMs instead of Normal or Classic BOMs.

Version BOMs cannot be created in the SAP S/4HANA classic GUI transaction codes (e.g. CS01). Instead, they must be created in the “Maintain Bill of Material” Fiori App (F1813). The BOM details and fields are exactly the same; the only difference between BOM structure of PEO and PP is the version number. 

The following table contains the main differences between classic and version-controlled BOMs:

Image 1. Main differences between Classic BOMs and Version BOMs

 

Management of Version-Controlled BOMs

SAP PEO provides a range of features for handing over complex product structures to manufacturing with version-controlled BOMs, which can be very useful for the usage in prototyping and new design implementation. With SAP PEO, three different BOMs can be used for the same finished product, based on the engineering requirements and process complexity:

  • Engineering BOM (EBOM)
  • Planning BOM (PBOM)
  • Shop Floor BOM or Manufacturing BOM (MBOM)

The classic production engineering, planning, and execution process is sequential. Production engineering starts after product design was completed. Production planning starts after release of MBOM and Shop Floor Routing. Production execution can start only after engineering, planning, and the procurement of components is completed. Sequential processing leads to long overall lead times, typical of PEO scenarios.

Usually, the first version BOM you create in an SAP PEO process is an EBOM. EBOM is the engineering bill of material which comes from the engineering department of the new product. We can create EBOM in SAP manually with snapshots or directly without snapshots in the “Maintain Bill of Material” Fiori App (F1813).

The EBOM can be first incorporated into a PBOM, which is then used to create the MBOM automatically. PBOMs can be used in production versions, which are used by MRP. Differentiating between PBOM and MBOM, and between Planning Routing and Shop Floor Routing, has the following advantages:

  1. It helps reduce lead time.
  2. It provides tools and a process to translate Phantom BOMs used in engineering and production planning into flat MBOMs.

Phantom BOMs allow the use of the same set of components in several parent BOMs, improving the consistency across different parent BOMs. When the parent material's BOM is exploded, the components in the main assembly and the components in any nested phantom assemblies are added to the flat component list of the MBOM. Phantoms can only be added to EBOMs and PBOMs. When converting the PBOM into a MBOM, the BOM gets flattened.

Image 2. BOM explosion with flattened phantom components

 

Maintain Bill of Material

As mentioned before, Version BOMs cannot be created using classic GUI BOM transaction codes. Version-controlled BOMs can be managed in the “Maintain Bill of Material” Fiori App (F1813). This app is not a PEO specific application but can be used for all BOM types in SAP S/4HANA.

With this app, you can display and manage all your BOMs. Using the available filters, you can find and view existing BOMs, drill down to the level of detail you need, and perform any necessary actions (such as delete BOMs, obsolete BOMs or assign change records to BOMs).

You can create new BOMs (whether classical, version-based, or alternative BOMs) and insert the relevant components along with the required data, including any attachments. This must be done one by one material, and the following options are available for version-controlled BOMs:

  • “Create Version BOM”: Create the initial version BOM for a material
  • “Create Version”: Create a new version of an existing version BOM
  • “Copy BOM”: Copy BOM components into a new initial version BOM for a material, to reduce manual maintenance
  • “Create Alternative”: Copy BOM components into a new initial version BOM for a material, with a new alternative BOM.

When adding components, you have the following options (not exhaustive):

  • You can add new lines one by one
  • You can use “Add Multiple Rows” button, which allows to add several rows info at the same time (10 lines)
  • You can also use existing lines as a template by using button “Copy”

Image 4. “Maintain Bill of Material” Fiori App (F1813), header data.

Image 5. “Maintain Bill of Material” Fiori App (F1813), BOM items.

Image 6. “Maintain Bill of Material” Fiori App (F1813), BOM details.

 

Migrate Classic BOMs to Version-Controlled BOMs

In case you are working with classic BOMs, and you would like to use your existing classic BOMs in SAP PEO, you can perform a one-time migration to convert your classic BOMs into version-controlled BOMs. PEO provides for that the migration report RMPE_MIGRATE_BOM. Please notice that this report is not designed for continuous migration of classic BOMs created in transaction CS01; the migration should be performed only once.

Image 7. Selection screen for classic BOMs migration report.

On the selection screen you can enter the BOMs which you want to convert to version-controlled BOMs. You can specify the target BOM as well, as in which do you want to create. And you have an option to delete the existing classic BOM after the creation of the Version BOM. Please notice that this migration report cannot be used for configurable materials.

More details are available on SAP Help Portal, on PEO Implementation Guide section.

 

Standard APIs for BOM Version Management

The below Remote-enabled Function Modules (RFC) are available and can be used for manipulation of version BOMs:

  • MPE_VERSNBOM_CREATE_INITIAL: Creates initial BOM version (first version)
  • MPE_VERSNBOM_CREATE_VERSION: Creates new BOM version (with reference)
  • MPE_VERSNBOM_UPDATE: Updates BOM version
  • MPE_VERSNBOM_READ: Reads BOM version
  • MPE_VERSNBOM_CHANGE_STATUS: Changes BOM version status

These APIs support BOM for both Unitized and Non-Unitized Materials, so the effectivity parameters for every BOM item are also supported. If these APIs were not available on your system, you can check SAP note 2855536 “Remote enabled Function Modules (RFC) for Manipulating Version Controlled Material Bill of Materials (BOM)”.

 

Customer Use Case for Massive Creation of Version BOMs

Customer works with version-controlled PBOMs and MBOMs. PBOMs are mainly used to flatten the phantom components. For some minor simple products, MBOMs can be directly created, but the standard procedure consists of first creating the PBOM, and later creating the MBOM by updating the PBOM on a Change Record (CR).

Customer manufactures highly engineered discrete products which contain hundreds of components, so requirement was to be able to create new version BOMs by importing the data from an Excel file. Thus, users could manage the BOM changes more quickly in Excel. As standard SAP functionalities do not offer this possibility, a custom program was developed.

Basic data requested by customer to be maintained at component level was item category (only stock items), item number, component, component quantity (always basic unit of measure), special procurement type, production supply area, and effectivity parameters (several values possible). The main key requirements to be considered were:

  1. Only creation of new versioned BOMs, not to change data on existing or just created BOMs.
  2. The new BOM versions are only created, but not released.
  3. The BOM release process to be manually done after the BOM creation, on “Maintain Bill of Material” Fiori App (F1813). This would also help as a double-check for the BOM upload.
  4. Keep component links with Operation Activities from the previous BOM version.

For the creation of the initial BOM version, that is, the first BOM version for the material, plant, BOM usage, and BOM alternative, the API MPE_VERSNBOM_CREATE_INITIAL was used. For the creation of a new BOM version, that is, the following successive version of existing version for the material, plant, BOM usage, and BOM alternative, the API MPE_VERSNBOM_CREATE_VERSION was used. In this latest case, the new BOM version is created with reference to an existing released BOM version. Let’s call it the Previous BOM version.

SAP PEO manages version-controlled routings, called Shop Floor Routings (SFR), that are not dependent on time and contain more level of detail. Every SFR operation can have one or multiple Operation Activities (OAs). OAs enable to define detailed work instructions for production operators without making scheduling and costing on operation level more complicated. You can also assign qualifications and buyoffs to OAs. In addition, you can create any-order groups (any sequence) or simultaneous groups (parallel).

Components can be assigned to the more detailed OA level. On “Component Assignment Workspace” of the SFR, users can perform component assignment to OAs in the produce segment for the currently selected MBOM and unassign components. The true challenge of this custom massive creation program was to keep the same component links with the OAs of the SFR, as per the Previous BOM version for still existing BOM items in the new version. This was a mandatory requirement for customer, as minor changes are normally done within the hundreds of components of the BOM, and if existing links are lost this would mean to manually re-assign all the components again.

Technically, the component links are maintained though the tables STPO (field STPO- STVKN_VERSN, inherited node number of BOM item) and STPO_LINKS. To maintain this link, you need the field BOM item node number (STPO-STLKN) to be imported for those BOM items for which you want to keep the same component links to OAs. Thus, you can assign by code the same value for fields STLKN, STPOZ, and STVKN in the “T_BOM_ITEMS_IN” structure, for the items with update type “U”. For the new items or those not keeping the links, the update type will be “I” and unique identifier will be required for field NEW_ITEM_IDENT.

 

Practical Example

Let’s review a practical example to verify how component links are kept. Originally the “Component Assignment Workspace” of the SFR for the assembled material is the following:

Image 8. Component assignation for existing BOM version as reference.

We create a new PBOM version for the assembled material, release it, assign a CR, update the new MBOM version, and release it. On the new PBOM version, we changed on Excel the quantity of one BOM item, the effectivity of another, and we have added a new component item. NOTE: These changes can also be performed on the PBOM of a phantom assembly component, and components will also be linked (by means of table STPO_LINKS).

With the new MBOM version, on the “Component Assignment Workspace” of the SFR for the assembled material we can notice the following:

  1. For those components with a change on the quantity, the assignation status is “Partially Assigned” (yellow).
  2. For those components with a change on the effectivity parameters, the assignation status is “Assigned Outside Effectivity” (red).
  3. For those components without any of above changes or not changed at all, the assignation status is “Assigned” (green).
  4. For the new components there is no assignation (no icon).

Image 9. Component assignation for new BOM version created.

 

Programs for Exporting/Importing Version BOMs to/from Excel

Initially, the standard exporting functionality provided by “Manage Bill of Materials” Fiori App (F1813) was used. But we found the following issues:

  • We were not able to include the header data on the exported Excel.
  • We couldn’t split the parameter effectivity data into several columns.
  • We couldn’t split the parameter effectivity data into several lines, in case multiple effectivity values exist.

Image 10. Exporting BOM items on “Maintain Bill of Material” Fiori App (F1813).

These issues were causing users to have to manually change and adapt the layout for later data importing, thus reducing the process agility because of the different download and upload layouts. Therefore, customer decided to also create a new custom export program to export the existing BOM versions of materials in an Excel file, directly with the same agreed layout to be later used for importing and creating a new BOM version. Thus, users would make the required changes for the BOMs directly on the Excel file, without the need of changing any layout.

The flow chart of the final solution for massive exporting and importing version BOMs is the following:

Image 11. Flow chart of the final solution for massive exporting and importing version BOMs

The selection screen and output list of the new program to export version BOMs to Excel are displayed on below images:

Image 12. Selection screen for program to export version BOMs to Excel.

Image 13. Output list for program to export version BOMs to Excel.

Columns colored in orange are the ones to be exported to Excel, that is, the ones which have been agreed to later be imported from the Excel to create the new BOM version (agreed layout). Rest of columns are calculated fields (basically descriptions). The implemented stripped Zebra pattern uses the same color for all the lines belonging to the same material BOM version (same plant, material, usage, alternative, and BOM version), to provide a visual help for fast identification of the components of the same BOM.

The exportation to Excel format is a customized feature. We have a header row in bold grey and several rows with the data values. Key fields of the different BOM levels (header, item, item effectivity) are on italics. The layout to be shared by both programs is the following:

Image 14. Excel layout to be shared by both programs.

The selection screen and output list of the new program to import version BOMs from Excel are displayed on below images:

Image 15. Selection screen for program to import version BOMs from Excel.

Image 16. Output list for program to import version BOMs from Excel.

Before creation of the new BOM versions can take place, several verifications are done to assure the data imported is correct. Basically, the existence of the different objects (plant, assembled material, component, item category, special procurement type, production supply area, model) is verified, together with some other additional checks.

Both APIs MPE_VERSNBOM_CREATE_INITIAL and MPE_VERSNBOM_CREATE_VERSION used for new BOM version creation allow execution without commit, so there are two possible execution modes:

  • Test Mode: the BOM creation is simulated, and all the messages returned by the API are displayed on the execution log, but BOMs are not saved on the database.
  • Execution Mode: the BOMs are saved on the database.

With this program more than one version BOM can be uploaded each time, thus increasing the agility of the users working with BOM management. For the performance’s sake of the program, there is a limit of records on the imported Excel file, delimited by the field “Maximum Records”, which can be customized as desired.

After the execution process, the execution log is displayed, but it is also saved as a specific application log to be later monitored in case of issues.

Image 17. Execution log for program to import version BOMs from Excel.

Image 18. Application log for program to import version BOMs from Excel.

entry.title: Import Job taking significant time to import single record via Odata services?
entry.content:
Hi,

I have created an Odata connection to import data from Datasphere to SAC. There is only single record in the view. When I'm trying to import the data via this Odata connection into my SAC model. The job is taking around 50 -60 seconds to refresh the data. There are no transformation logic defined in this import job. It is one to one mapping with the dimensions of SAC model. I'm wondering just to pull a single record why is this import job taking so much time. Anyone who has faced this similar kind of issue or knows the reason for this delay. Their comments would be deeply appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: 'Please Enter a type' error is coming while selecting product from the material OVS
entry.content:
'Please Enter a type' error is coming while selecting product from the material OVS.

 

I have created below SDK field in ActivityManagement xbo. 

Created a EC and added that SDK field to that EC view. EC is configured and showing in the screen also.

Now i changed want to use the field as a material value help.

I have used Public OVS: /BYD_COD/SalesOnDemand/Product/UI/COD_Material_SOVS.OVS.uicomponent

I did this below configuration:

 

Please help me what i am missing there....

SAP Cloud Applications Studio C4C Extensibility

entry.title: Re: Let's get to know each other!
entry.content:
Hi Samuel, 

I am quite new to this forum and trying to find my way through the maze. I am looking for some support about existing Plug & Gain for SAP Signavio Intelligence covering the Vendor invoice approval process once Invoice data have been validated. There is one which is installed covering the VIM Open Text piece of the process (i.e. Invoice validation), not the invoice approval till invoice posting workflow. Here are my questions:

1. Is there a specific forum I should reach to address such request (and if yes, can you help?

2. If there is no such forum on VIM Plug & gain, who should I contact regarding data file transformation and load format, should I decide to build my own Investigation dashboard?

Thanks

Carl

entry.title: Re: Feature Control in RAP, Static Feature Control in RAP, Part - 1
entry.content: @Krishna_karale Thanks for the information. Can you also post some examples for handling field property of a table in RAP (both static and dynamic). static - complete column of one field and Dynamic - based on value of one field in same table other field value should be determined.

entry.title: ECT-226339 - New Experience people profile rating numbers not labels
entry.content:
In the Performance Rating portlet, we are seeing the numerical rating presented, not the label as previously presented.  The first image shows the initial view from the profile, the second shows the "View All" view from the profile.  Is there a setting or adjustment that can be made to show the label in the initial view as well and NOT the numerical rating?

Thank you,

TracyK (EIR)
